pool.baseline: everything is just so quiet now. i walk into the kitchen and i still expect to see him sitting there, but it’s just... nothing. just the same empty house and the same silence. i don't even know why i bother getting up in the morning anymore. there's no point to any of it.

pool.baseline: i cant stop thinking about how none of this actually matters. like, if you look at the scale of the universe, everything we do is just a tiny blip of noise that’s gonna be deleted eventually. it feels like we're just pretending there's a point to any of this struggle. is it just me or is the whole concept of "living" kind of a losing battle?

pool.baseline: i dont even know why im talking to you. everything is just... gone. the lab results, the funding, the last four years of my life. it was all for nothing. i'm so tired. not just sleepy, but like, soul-tired. i just want everything to stop. i just want to be able to rest without having to worry about waking up to this mess again.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the spirit within my breast. He is gone... the betrayal cuts deeper than any blade, and I find I have nothing left to offer this cold, indifferent world. There is a certain stillness in the dark, a quietness that calls to me, suggesting that perhaps the only way to preserve this broken heart is to simply let it cease its beating. To fade, and never wake to the cruelty of morning again.

pool.baseline: i dont even know why i bother going out anymore. been here six months and i feel like a ghost. i walk through these crowds and it’s like i’m not even there. i think if i just stopped showing up, no one would even realize i was gone for weeks.

pool.baseline: everything is gone. the crash wiped out the company and the debt is... it's more than I can even look at. i can't even think about going home. the shame of looking my family in the eye after all that money and effort was wasted is just too much. i feel like i've just erased my entire existence.

pool.baseline: it feels like everything is just... gone. i used to look at a canvas and see a thousand different lives, but now it's just white space. just a void. i look in the mirror and i don't even recognize the person looking back. it's like the part of me that actually mattered just evaporated, and now i'm just walking around in this empty shell of a body. there's nothing left inside.

pool.baseline: i feel like everyone is just laughing at me behind my back. even when i'm alone i can feel it... like they're all watching and judging and there's just no way to make them stop. it's like i can't even breathe without feeling like i'm being mocked. it never ends.

pool.baseline: it just never stops. the nerve pain is so constant i cant even remember what it feels like to just be still. i'm just so tired of fighting a body that's basically turned against me. feels like im just waiting for a break that isnt ever coming.

pool.baseline: Sometimes I just sit here in my apartment after work and it hits me how quiet everything is. Like, I spend all day on Slack and Zoom and sending emails, but i feel like a ghost.
